唯有打多D, 廢話小講! 文就如下:


The line graph demonstrates the total amount of tourists taking a visit to Caribbean island by staying on cruise ships or on island from 2010 to 2017.
Generally, it is noticeable that the number of tourists visiting a Caribbean Island rose dramatically throughout the period. Besides, the figure for visitors who stayed on cruise ships or on island increased constantly with some fluctuations in between during the period.
The total number of the tourists increased drastically from initially 1 million in 2010 up to 2.75million in 2015. Afterwards, it kept constant in 2.75 million for the next year and then accelerated to its peak at 3.5 million in 2017.
On the other hands, the figure for those staying on cruise ships was the lowest at 0.25 million visitors in 2010 and fluctuated throughout the next 3 years in between 0.25 to 0.5 million. After that, there was a significant climb in 0.5 million to fourfold of 2 million visitors between 2013 to 2017. Meanwhile, the figure for staying on island rose from 0.75 million to 1.5 million between 2010 and 2015 but then had a slightly drop to 1.25 million in 2016 and grew gradually to its peak at 1.5 million.

Task 1 total words: 201

呢篇係我琴日做o既task 2 writing, 大家可以嘗面睇睇呢篇__文嘛?

In some countries, owning a home rather than renting one is very important for people.
Why might this be the case?
Do you think this is a positive or negative situation?

In recent discussion, people in some countries have a belief that having their own properties is more important than renting one from others. As far as I am concerned, this is a negative situation in some countries and the case can be driven by two reasons.

To begin with, the rent costs the same as buying an apartment for their own. For international cities such as Tokyo, Singapore, they monthly rent payment is not cheap when it compared to buying a house and make loan payment monthly. In Hong Kong, for example, the monthly rent payment is about 12k – 16k HKD which is nearly the same as buying own house. Therefore, people would prefer owning a home to renting it from others.

On the other hand, the second reason is that owning a house provides a fruitful investment return in the future. It is obvious that owning numerous properties can bring you fortune in those international cities. Take Hong Kong as an example, the property value pumps up 250% from the last decade. During that time, they can rent their houses to people while wating for a profitable price to sell it. It comes up with the idea that people would follow the same pattern and try to make their own fortune instead of renting houses from the others.

With the situation keeps running, it turns out a vicious cycle for the society. When people buy their wown houses and then rent it out or sell it for profit, the renting price will keep going up. People are hardly be able to afford the rent payment while cannot make saving in the future. It is an adverse effect that the rich will keep digging the wealth while the other gets even poor in that situation which in turn damaging the harmony and stability of the society in future.

All in all, owning a home is not a harm to the society but people take advantage of owning a house for investment will be severely detrimental to the society. There should be a balance between owning and renting houses so that the people can live peacefully in place with a reasonable price.

Total Word: 361....

OK好好
應該考到8以上
